frostbite.thesis: Attractive pond (structural convenience growth) and a strong horse (#2 with the biggest private-label margin gap to close).

sector.name: European convenience & private-label grocery
sector.summary: Convenience formats structurally outgrowing the broader grocery market; private-label penetration rising as consumers trade down.

competitive.label: #2 in DACH convenience by share
competitive.moat: Dense store network + own-brand supply chain; loyalty dataset under-monetised.

views.view: "European Food Retail": convenience the clear structural winner; private-label the primary margin lever.
Rates the listed leader Overweight; frames the margin gap the #2 could close under new ownership.
Former category buyer confirms 6–8pt gross-margin uplift is achievable by lifting own-brand mix toward the leader.

gridsense.thesis: High-growth pond (grid modernisation) with a genuinely defensible horse (proprietary sensor data), but early-stage.

sector.name: Grid-optimisation / energy-transition software
sector.summary: Grid modernisation and renewables integration driving durable demand for optimisation software; proprietary telemetry is the scarce input.

competitive.label: Niche leader in sensor-data grid AI
competitive.moat: Proprietary sensor network + multi-year telemetry — hard to replicate; not a GPT wrapper.

peers.name: Hyperscaler platforms
peers.note: Horizontal risk — the key watch item

views.view: "Energy Transition Software": structural multi-year tailwind; proprietary-data players command a premium.
A national-TSO buyer validates the data moat: "the sensor network is the part competitors can't copy."
Places the category in "emerging, high-growth"; flags hyperscaler entry as the principal risk.

meridian.thesis: Improving pond (reshoring tailwind) and a solid niche horse; customer concentration is the one thing to underwrite.

sector.name: European precision components
sector.summary: OEMs dual-sourcing away from tariff-exposed regions; DACH precision suppliers are direct beneficiaries of reshoring.

competitive.label: #3–4 regionally, strong niche
competitive.moat: Qualified supplier status with sticky OEM relationships; hard to switch mid-programme.

views.view: "Reshoring Winners": DACH precision suppliers among the clearest beneficiaries of supply-chain de-risking.
An OEM procurement lead confirms an active dual-sourcing shift toward European suppliers — durable, not cyclical.

alpine.thesis: Scarce-capacity pond with pricing power; sponsor-era leverage is the underwriting question, not the demand.

sector.name: Temperature-controlled logistics (cold-chain 3PL)
sector.summary: Pharma cold-chain and grocery e-commerce driving durable demand against constrained capacity.

competitive.label: Regional #2 by capacity
competitive.moat: Scarce temperature-controlled capacity in key catchments — hard to replicate quickly.

views.view: "Logistics": cold chain commands a premium to ambient on scarcity and pricing power.
An ex-operator notes utilisation is high and new-build lead times are long — supportive of pricing.

verde.thesis: Weakening pond (DTC normalisation) and a losing horse — this is a special-situations / value case, not a quality compounder.

sector.name: DTC home goods
sector.summary: Demand pulled forward during COVID is unwinding; category soft with elevated customer-acquisition costs.

competitive.label: Mid-tier, losing share
competitive.moat: Brand + loyalty data, but eroding under CAC pressure.

views.view: Direct coverage: downgraded on covenant risk and weakening trading; flags liquidity as the binding constraint.
Direct coverage: sees a strategic-review / take-private path as the most likely resolution.

brauhaus.thesis: Flat-to-soft pond (beer volumes) offset by a strong regional-brand horse; premiumisation is the value lever.

sector.name: European beer & regional brewing
sector.summary: Volume headwinds in mainstream beer offset by premium/craft resilience and strong local-brand loyalty.

competitive.label: Regional brand leader
competitive.moat: Entrenched local brand, cash-generative, strong regional distribution.

views.view: "Beverages": cautious on mainstream volumes; premium and strong local brands the resilient pockets.
A former distributor confirms the local brand's pricing power and loyal on-trade base.

nordfiber.thesis: Strong tailwind pond (plastics substitution) and an ESG-aligned, improving horse — a clean quality profile.

sector.name: Sustainable fibre-based packaging
sector.summary: Regulatory push toward fibre-based packaging is a multi-year substitution tailwind.

competitive.label: ESG-aligned regional #2
competitive.moat: Fibre expertise + regulatory alignment; improving margins.

views.view: "Packaging & ESG": fibre-based substitution a structural winner as regulation tightens.
A brand-owner sustainability lead confirms accelerating switching from plastic to fibre formats.
